title: Bhubaneswari Temple
tagline: Bhubaneswari Temple — spiritual town of Tripura

overview.short: Bhubaneswari Temple is a Hindu temple located in Tripura, India, in Rajnagar (55 km from Agartala).
overview.description: Bhubaneswari Temple is a Hindu temple located in Tripura, India, in Rajnagar (55 km from Agartala). Believed to be built in 1660–1675 AD, it is located next to the palace built by Maharaja Govinda Manikya. The temple is mentioned in Rabindranath Tagore's work.

topPlaces.name: Bijoy sagar
topPlaces.description: Bijoy Sagar is a lake in Udaipur in North-East India. It is also called Mahadeb Dighi.

topPlaces.name: Udaipur, Tripura
topPlaces.description: Udaipur (Bengali pronunciation: [udɔe̯pur]), formerly known as Rangamati, is the third biggest urban area in the Indian state of Tripura. The city was the capital of the state during the reign of the Manikya dynasty.

topPlaces.name: ONGC Tripura Power Company
topPlaces.description: ONGC Tripura Power Company Limited (OTPC) also known as Palatana Power Plant, is a thermal power plant station in Palatana, Udaipur in the Gomoti district of the northeastern Indian state of Tripura. It is a division of Oil and Natural Gas Corporation which is under the ownership of Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, Government of India.

items.note: Bijoy Sagar is a lake in Udaipur in North-East India. It is also called Mahadeb Dighi.

Udaipur (Bengali pronunciation: [udɔe̯pur]), formerly known as Rangamati, is the third biggest urban area in the Indian state of Tripura. The city was the capital of the state during the reign of the Manikya dynasty.

ONGC Tripura Power Company Limited (OTPC) also known as Palatana Power Plant, is a thermal power plant station in Palatana, Udaipur in the Gomoti district of the northeastern Indian state of Tripura. It is a division of Oil and Natural Gas Corporation which is under the ownership of Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, Government of India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bhubaneswari Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bhubaneswari Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bhubaneswari Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Maharaja Bir Bikram Airport, Agartala (47 km) and the nearest railway station is Agartala Railway Station (44 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bhubaneswari Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bhubaneswari Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bhubaneswari Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Bhubaneswari Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bhubaneswari Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bijoy sagar, Udaipur, Tripura, ONGC Tripura Power Company.
